teaching_card.title: Subject-Verb Agreement (Hard)
teaching_card.body: A verb must match its subject in number. Ignore words between the subject and verb — especially prepositional phrases — which often hide the true subject and bait you into the wrong number.
key_points: Find the true subject, ignoring in-between phrases.
Singular subject → singular verb; plural → plural.

screens.title: Match the verb to the subject
screens.body: A verb agrees with its subject in number: a singular subject takes a singular verb, a plural subject a plural verb. The trick is finding the real subject — it's often not the noun closest to the verb.
key_points: Singular subject -> singular verb; plural -> plural.
Find the true subject before choosing the verb.

screens.title: Ignore what's in between
screens.body: Phrases between the subject and verb — especially 'of …' phrases — are there to bait you. Mentally cross them out: 'The box of old tools (is/are)' -> 'The box … is.' Also know that 'each,' 'every,' and 'neither' are singular even though they feel plural.
key_points: Cross out 'of the …' phrases, then check.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: 'The collection of rare coins (was/were) donated.' The subject is 'collection' (singular), not 'coins,' so the verb is 'was.' The plural 'coins' right before the verb is the trap.
screens.worked_example: The list of winners WAS posted (subject = list, not winners).
